location: On the floor of a small cave network in Stone Coffin Fissure. From the Fissure Cross site of grace, head northwest to a giant stone coffin tipped nearly vertical, climb down to a ledge running beneath the brick platform near the grace, and follow the cave to its far end.

source.name: cave floor pickup, Stone Coffin Fissure

tactics: Stone Coffin Fissure itself is sealed until Miquella's Great Rune is broken — the entrance chasm southwest of Cerulean Coast won't open before then
From Fissure Cross grace, go northwest to the nearly-vertical giant coffin and climb down to the ledge underneath the brick platform
The small cave holding the sword is guarded by several putrescent oozes — they're slow individually but can box you in inside the tight cave, so pull them apart rather than getting surrounded

Don't confuse this with the base-game Sword of St. Trina found in Caelid — this DLC version applies eternal (unrecoverable) sleep instead of regular sleep, has longer reach, and a higher crit modifier
lore: Once St. Trina's plain silver blade, its color changed to deep velvet after she was abandoned — the faint lavender mist she once gave off is said to have curdled into a thicker, intoxicating purple cloud that puts anyone caught in it into a sleep from which they cannot wake.

builds.name: INT/DEX sleep-proc build
builds.why: High Dexterity and Intelligence scaling plus a 42 sleep buildup and the ranged Mists of Eternal Sleep skill make it a strong status-application straight sword for magic-leaning characters

builds.name: Hybrid physical-magic poke
builds.why: Splits high base damage between physical and magic, giving an Intelligence build a fast melee option that still benefits from Int investment alongside sorceries
